plan: Read existing tokens.css, global.css, and shell.css to understand current token coverage and identify gaps against acceptance criteria (color palette completeness, typography scale, spacing scale, shared card style, shared button style)

Expand src/styles/tokens.css with complete color palette: add semantic color tokens (--color-success, --color-warning, --color-danger for severity badges), shadow tokens (--shadow-sm, --shadow-md for card elevation), border-radius tokens (--radius-sm, --radius-md, --radius-lg), and transition tokens (--transition-fast, --transition-base) while preserving all existing tokens from US-004

Expand typography scale in tokens.css: add --font-size-xs (0.75rem), --font-size-2xl (2rem), --font-weight-normal (400), --font-weight-medium (500), --font-weight-bold (600), --line-height-tight (1.25), --line-height-base (1.5), --line-height-relaxed (1.75), and --letter-spacing-tight/-wide tokens
Verify spacing scale completeness in tokens.css: current scale is xs(4px) through 2xl(48px) — add --spacing-3xl (64px) if needed for section gaps, confirm 4px-base progression is consistent

Create src/styles/card.css with shared .card component style: white/surface background, border using --color-border, border-radius using token, box-shadow for subtle elevation, padding using --spacing-md/--spacing-lg, and responsive adjustments — this is the panel/card used across all dashboard modules

Create src/styles/button.css with shared .btn base style and variants: .btn-primary (accent green background, white text), .btn-secondary (outlined/ghost style), .btn-icon (icon-only compact), with proper focus-visible states, hover/active transitions, disabled state, and keyboard accessibility
Update src/styles/global.css to import card.css and button.css so they are available globally, and add base heading styles (h1-h4) using the expanded typography tokens

Create tests/us-005/theme-tokens.test.ts: verify all color palette tokens are defined on :root, verify typography scale tokens exist, verify spacing scale tokens exist, verify card styles can be applied and produce expected computed styles, verify button styles and variants work correctly
Run npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm run test to validate all code passes quality gates and existing US-004 tests still pass
Fix any lint, typecheck, or test failures discovered in the previous step
testing_recommendations: Unit test: verify all CSS custom properties (color, typography, spacing) are defined on :root element by parsing tokens.css
Unit test: verify .card class applies expected background, border, border-radius, padding, and box-shadow computed styles
Unit test: verify .btn and .btn-primary/.btn-secondary classes apply correct colors, padding, border-radius, and cursor styles
Unit test: verify button focus-visible states exist for keyboard accessibility
Unit test: verify typography heading styles (h1-h4) use correct font-size and font-weight tokens
Regression test: run existing US-004 responsive-shell tests to confirm no breakage from token expansion
Run full suite: npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm run test before marking complete
Visual spot-check: confirm card and button styles render correctly in dev server (npm run dev)
risks: Expanding tokens.css could break existing shell.css if any token names are changed rather than added — must only add new tokens, never rename existing ones
New card.css and button.css global imports increase CSS bundle size, though impact is minimal for a static site
Typography scale expansion must not conflict with existing font-size-sm/base/lg/xl tokens already used in shell.css
Test assertions on computed CSS values can be fragile in jsdom — may need to test CSS file content/parsing rather than computed styles for reliability
Shared card/button styles must remain generic enough to work across all future modules (fingerprint receipt, OSINT cards, permission audit, etc.) without being too opinionated
